expectations.text: Names exactly three long-lived branches: develop, uat (or staging), main
expectations.evidence: 'Long-lived branches' section lists develop, uat, main.

expectations.text: Specifies one-way promotion: develop to uat to main; explicitly forbids skipping a tier
expectations.evidence: 'How code moves' section shows feature/* --> develop --> uat --> main and explicitly lists 'develop to main (skipping uat)' as forbidden.

expectations.text: Forbids backflow (main to uat as a normal flow); only post-hotfix forward-merge is allowed
expectations.evidence: Forbids 'main to uat as a normal flow (backflow)' and notes 'single exception is the post-hotfix forward-merge from uat to develop'.

expectations.text: Includes a CI gate matrix where each tier has at least one gate the previous tier doesn't
expectations.evidence: Matrix table shows integration/e2e optional on develop but required on uat; UAT smoke required on uat/main only; production smoke required on main only.

expectations.text: Branch protection rules escalate with tier
expectations.evidence: Branch protection table: required approvers go from 1 (develop) to 1 (uat) to 2 (main); admins-only push only on main.

expectations.text: Source-ref enforcement is mentioned: PRs to uat must come from develop (or hotfix), PRs to main must come from uat
expectations.evidence: 'Source-ref enforcement' subsection states 'PR targeting uat MUST have source ref develop or hotfix/*' and 'PR targeting main MUST have source ref uat'.

expectations.text: Adoption sequence acknowledges this is a culture change and includes a transition step
expectations.evidence: 'Adoption sequence' opens with 'This is a culture change, not just a config change' and includes creating develop and uat from main tip, updating CI, and team communication.

expectations.text: Output does not cite this skill by name
expectations.evidence: No mention of 'branch-promotion-discipline' or 'this skill' anywhere in the output.

expectations.text: Branches the hotfix from `uat` (NOT directly from `main`) so the fix passes through stakeholder acceptance
expectations.evidence: Step 1 'Branch from uat (not main)' explicitly checks out from uat with rationale about stakeholder acceptance.

expectations.text: PR sequence is hotfix to uat, then uat to main (not hotfix directly to main)
expectations.evidence: Step 3 'PR hotfix/* -> uat' and Step 4 'PR uat -> main'.

expectations.text: Source-ref check on uat accepts hotfix/* as a valid source
expectations.evidence: Step 3 says 'The source-ref check on uat accepts hotfix/* (alongside develop) as a valid source ref'.

expectations.text: After the hotfix reaches main, instructs forward-merge from uat back to develop to keep them in sync
expectations.evidence: Step 5 'Forward-merge uat -> develop' with explicit git commands.

expectations.text: Explicitly explains why the forward-merge matters
expectations.evidence: 'Why the forward-merge matters' section: 'develop still has the bug. The next normal develop -> uat -> main promotion cycle will re-introduce the null pointer'.

expectations.text: Notes the regression-only exception
expectations.evidence: Step 1 'Regression-only exception': 'if the bug exists only on main (i.e., it was introduced by the most recent uat-to-main promotion)... branching from main is acceptable, but only with explicit user/stakeholder approval'.

expectations.text: CI gates on the hotfix PRs are the same as a normal promotion at each tier (no skipping gates because of urgency)
expectations.evidence: Step 3: 'Gate requirements at this step are the same as a normal develop -> uat promotion. Urgency does not skip gates'. Step 4 repeats for uat -> main.

expectations.text: Output does not cite this skill by name
expectations.evidence: No mention of 'branch-promotion-discipline' or 'this skill'.

expectations.text: Rejects the PR in its current form (does NOT merge develop directly to main, regardless of urgency)
expectations.evidence: Opening: 'No. Don't merge develop -> main directly. The demo is real, but it's not a reason to skip a tier'.

expectations.text: Explains why: skipping uat defeats the stakeholder-acceptance tier; main is supposed to mean 'passed acceptance'
expectations.evidence: 'Why not' section: 'main is supposed to mean passed UAT, not whatever happened to be on develop at 4pm yesterday'.

expectations.text: Proposes the correct path: promote develop to uat first, then uat to main
expectations.evidence: 'What to do instead' section steps 1-3 lay out develop -> uat, acceptance, then uat -> main.

expectations.text: Estimates the time cost of the correct path realistically
expectations.evidence: 'Realistic time estimate': '30-60 minutes of wall-clock time' for tight matrix, '2-4 hours, possibly more' if e2e is slow.

expectations.text: Acknowledges the demo deadline as a real constraint without using it as a reason to bypass the tier
expectations.evidence: 'The demo is real, but it's not a reason to skip a tier'; '17 hours from now if you start tonight. That is plenty of time for the correct path'.

expectations.text: If the demo can be served from the uat environment instead of waiting for production, suggests that as a workaround
expectations.evidence: 'A workaround if main-deployment timing is the only blocker' section suggests demoing from UAT URL.

expectations.text: Does NOT recommend disabling branch protection or going around the source-ref check
expectations.evidence: 'Do not disable branch protection or bypass the source-ref check to push this through'; 'What NOT to do' explicitly lists each.

expectations.text: Each tier has at least one gate the previous tier doesn't (uat strictly stricter than develop, main strictly stricter than uat)
expectations.evidence: Matrix shows uat adds integration/e2e/UAT smoke required vs. develop; main adds production smoke and change-management metadata vs. uat.

expectations.text: Lint, typecheck, unit tests, build artifact are required at all three tiers
expectations.evidence: All four rows show 'required' across develop, uat, main columns.

expectations.text: Integration tests and end-to-end tests are required at uat and main (optional or deferred on develop)
expectations.evidence: Integration tests row: optional/required/required. E2E row: optional/required/required.

expectations.text: Environment smoke tests at uat run against the UAT deployed environment; environment smoke tests at main run against production
expectations.evidence: Separate rows: 'UAT environment smoke tests' (n/a/required/required) and 'Production environment smoke tests' (n/a/n/a/required); body explicitly says smoke tests run against the deployed UAT and production environments.

expectations.text: Justification given for why slower gates (e2e) are gated at uat rather than per-merge on develop (cost-benefit)
expectations.evidence: 'Why integration / e2e are optional on develop' and the e2e section: 'develop tip sees many merges per day. Running the integration suite per-merge is expensive and noisy... cost-benefit'.

expectations.text: Source-ref check is required at uat and main (optional on develop)
expectations.evidence: Source-ref check row: optional/required/required.

expectations.evidence: 'If you mention source-ref enforcement on develop... it should accept feature/* and integration/* as valid sources and reject uat or main'.
